summary: Iteration 6 — verified the 'approved floor plans wiring' feature set end-to-end (Jan 2026). Backend pytest: 50/50 PASS across test_workflow_tweaks.py, test_phase_b_package_adjustment.py, test_phase_c_design_iteration.py, test_crm_phase_a.py in ~18s against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Frontend Playwright covered: (1) Designer login → 3 tabs in correct order [designer-tab-verifications, designer-tab-approved, designer-tab-projects]; (2) Approved Floor Plans tab renders, lists 9 approved verifications with floor-plan file links, NO email/mobile leaked, 'Open Design Project →' deep-link sets URL hash to '#projects?focus=<id>' and pre-selects the project in Active Projects (3D); (3) Customer (workflow-cust, phase=designing) sees design-started-banner + site-visit-picker + site-visit-submit-btn — after submitting 2026-07-20T15:00 the picker is replaced by site-visit-confirmed showing '7/20/2026, 3:00:00 PM' + Reschedule link, and a green toast 'Site visit scheduled. Our team will confirm shortly.' fires; (4) Admin → Active Designs (3D) → workflow-cust project header shows 'Site Visit: 7/20/2026, 3:00:00 PM' (data-testid=project-site-visit) along with full customer email+mobile (admin privacy intact). All 11 review_request items pass.

issues: Sidebar lists ~10 entries named just 'Customer' with empty project_name and 0 renders — these are leftover test-seed users from prior pytest runs (no teardown is by design per workflow_tweaks fixture). Not a bug from this iteration, but the production admin should not see anonymous 'Customer' rows. Consider hiding rows whose user has no project_name OR adding a one-off cleanup script.
Two 404s logged on /dashboard/admin first-load (asset fetches). Non-blocking — page renders and all tabs work — but worth chasing the missing resources in DevTools.

action_items: (carry-over from iter 5) AdminLogin.jsx line 12: default redirect '/admin' still points to a non-existent route — should be '/dashboard/admin'. Not retested this iteration; /login (role-based) flow continues to work.

OPTIONAL: move data-testid="site-visit-picker" from the wrapping <div> onto the actual <input type="datetime-local"> so Playwright/Cypress fill() works without an inner selector. Currently tests must use '[data-testid="site-visit-picker"] input[type="datetime-local"]'. Cosmetic.

OPTIONAL design polish: 'Designer Studio → Active Projects (3D)' sidebar shows ~10 anonymous 'Customer' rows (test-fixture residue). Filter rows where !project_name && renders.length===0, or run a cleanup of orphan test users.
critical_code_review_comments: admin_moderate_verification (server.py ~line 1177) correctly: (a) creates the design project when approving, (b) sets project_phase='designing', (c) clears site_visit_at, (d) returns design_project_id in the response — all asserted by test_approve_floor_plan_wires_design_and_site_visit + test_approve_auto_creates_design_project_and_advances_to_designing.

admin_list_verifications (~line 1160) now attaches design_project_id + site_visit_at to every record AND continues to strip customer.email/mobile for the designer role — verified for both list and detail endpoints.

schedule_site_visit (~line 1316) correctly 400s on empty string (test_site_visit_endpoint_rejects_empty PASS) and saves a clean ISO datetime to user.site_visit_at, echoed on GET /auth/me and visible to admin via /admin/verifications + /admin/design/projects.

DesignerDashboard.jsx 3-tab layout + ApprovedFloorPlans.jsx + DesignerProjects.jsx focus-by-hash all wire together cleanly. The hash deep-link survives a navigation, and the Active Projects sidebar pre-selects the matched project on mount.

CustomerDashboard.jsx designing-phase block (banner + picker + confirmed) is fully functional; the old 'scheduling'/'confirmed' phase block is now dead code (user explicitly chose not to remove it). Recommend cleanup in a future iteration to reduce CustomerDashboard.jsx size (currently grows past best-practice 700 lines).
test_credentials: Used admin@homesqre.com / Homesqre@2026, designer@homesqre.com / Staff@2026, workflow-cust@homesqre.com / Pass@123 (auto-seeded by test_workflow_tweaks.py fixture).
seed_data_creation: Reset workflow-cust@homesqre.com to project_phase='designing' + site_visit_at=null via direct mongo update before the customer-UI test, then submitted 2026-07-20T15:00 through the UI which persisted to user.site_visit_at. test_workflow_tweaks.py fixture continues to create workflow-cust + workflow-des on every run without teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Approved Floor Plans wiring is fully working: approve → backend creates design project + advances phase to 'designing' + clears site_visit_at; designer dashboard has 3 tabs with deep-link from Approved to Active Projects; customer designing-phase shows banner + datetime-local picker; admin sees the site-visit slot on the project header. Reproduce by logging in as designer@homesqre.com, clicking 'Approved Floor Plans', then 'Open Design Project →' on any row. Customer-side: login as workflow-cust@homesqre.com / Pass@123 (already in 'designing' phase after pytest run) — if site_visit_at is already set you'll see 'Site Visit Scheduled'; click 'Reschedule' to re-test the picker. Two minor design notes carried into action_items.
rca of the issue: User's original complaint ('approved floor plans not visible / not properly wired in designer dashboard') is fully resolved. Root cause was twofold: (a) approval handler used to set phase='scheduling' and leave design-project creation to a separate admin click; (b) DesignerDashboard had no surface for approved-but-not-yet-rendered work. Fix consolidates approval into one transactional step (project_phase='designing' + design project auto-created + site_visit_at cleared) and adds a dedicated ApprovedFloorPlans tab between Verifications and Active Projects with privacy-safe customer info + floor-plan downloads + a deep-link to the design project. Verified at API + UI level.
